title: STAT+: AbbVie to buy Apogee Therapeutics in nearly $11B deal
summary: AbbVie is buying Apogee Therapeutics, the developer of an experimental atopic dermatitis drug, for nearly $11B, continuing a run of pharma acquisitions.

title: STAT+: Definium LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in late-stage trial
summary: Definium Therapeutics said that its LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in its first Phase 3 trial, bringing the psychedelic drug closer to a potential approval.

title: Bionyra brings in $165M for next-generation immune drugs
summary: The biotech, co-founded by Sofinnova Partners and a former Sanofi immunology R&D head, is testing new biologics against common immune conditions.

title: STAT+: FDA reverses course on Regenxbio’s childhood gene therapy after rejection
summary: The FDA will reconsider approving Regenxbio's experimental gene therapy for a deadly and rare childhood brain disorder it rejected just four months ago.

title: From episodes to a lifetime: How AI can enable women’s health brands to build a partnership that lasts
summary: Women's health audiences have long memories and finely tuned instincts for what is performative.

title: The first 14 days: How specialty programs set patients up for success
summary: As specialty medicines play a growing role in healthcare, the patient journey that follows is equally critical.

title: Health equity researchers fear unseen level of scrutiny under White House proposal
summary: Health disparities researchers say a new White House proposal could disqualify much of their work from federal funding — perhaps the most serious threat yet to the future of their…

title: STAT+: Pharma goes on a spending spree, snapping up biotechs in a hurry
summary: Pharmaceutical companies are hungry to make deals, and they’re making more than they have in years.

title: Opinion: I work in a psychiatric ER. I’m watching the system fail people in real time
summary: The psychiatric ER is “a detox center for those with no safe place to withdraw and a respite center for caregivers at their breaking point,” writes a psychiatrist.

title: Opinion: STAT+: Enough, already: the problem with clinical trial data collection
summary: “Nearly 30% of the data collected in clinical trials do not directly inform key decisions, yet patients are still asked to provide it.”

title: STAT+: Sanofi names new R&D head as it tries to jump-start pipeline
summary: Sanofi said that Paulo Fontoura, the chief medical officer at Xaira, would be taking over as its head of R&D, as the company tries to jumpstart its pipeline.

title: Opinion: STAT readers debate blue zones, open-access publishing fees, and more
summary: Keep those letters coming!

title: Opinion: Why the space in ‘health care’ matters
summary: When STAT polled our readers on ‘health care’ vs. ‘healthcare,’ about 60% of our readers voted for one word.

title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

title: Federal grant delays could jeopardize essential disability services, research
summary: Disability researchers across the country worry about their jobs, and the future of their institutions, as a delay in issuing federal grants drags on.

title: Tennessee pharmacies sell potent ivermectin, led by anti-vaccine doctor who’s taken ‘bucketloads’
summary: Tennessee pharmacies are selling potent versions of the antiparasitic drug ivermectin, led by an anti-vaccine doctor who’s taken "bucketloads."

title: Opinion: STAT+: The real work for making dramatic gains against pancreatic cancer is just beginning
summary: The co-founders of Darwin Health write that the real work for making dramatic gains against pancreatic cancers is just beginning.

title: Opinion: Maternity deserts aren’t accidents. They’re the result of a design flaw
summary: Shrinking fertility should mean less strain on doctors. But nearly half of U.S. counties lack a practicing obstetrician or gynecologist.

title: STAT+: CMS recalculates Medicare Advantage star ratings again, adding more volatility to program
summary: Medicare Advantage plans that receive four out of five stars or better get bonus payments, which have ballooned to $16 billion this year.

title: STAT+: FDA advisory panel endorses Moderna mRNA flu vaccine that was subject of controversy
summary: A panel of advisers to FDA gave its endorsement to a seasonal mRNA flu vaccine that was developed by Moderna and that earlier this year became the subject of controversy.

title: Moderna flu vaccine wins unanimous support from FDA panel
summary: The endorsement of an advisory committee positions Moderna to complete a dramatic turnaround for a shot the FDA declined to review earlier this year.

title: STAT+: Eli Lilly ends 340B drug discounts to some hospitals for failing to provide claims data
summary: Making good on its threat, Eli Lilly has begun eliminating mandated price breaks to a few dozen hospitals that participate in a federal drug discount program.

title: New hope in treating Huntington’s disease and a report card on RFK Jr.’s promises
summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: new hope in treating Huntington's disease and a report card on RFK Jr.'s promises. Listen now.

title: Ebola cases increase almost 40% in a week as death toll passes 200
summary: Ebola cases have increased almost 40% in a week in the current outbreak in Congo and Uganda as the death toll passes 200.

title: cAMPfield lands $180M  to build gut disease drug
summary: The biotech is advancing an anti-inflammatory drug that originated with vTv for inflammatory bowel diseases, leaning on a better side effect profile.

title: Biogen adds more immune drugs in $1B buyout of secretive startup RayThera
summary: The companies didn’t specify the drugs Biogen is acquiring in the deal, though patents RayThera secured provide important clues.

title: Pfizer CFO to step down; Denali sells a regulatory fast-pass
summary: A search is underway for a successor to Dave Denton, who took a job outside the pharmaceutical industry. Elsewhere, a new kind of Duchenne gene therapy got venture backing and a GSK deal paid…

title: Kardigan extends biotech’s streak of big IPOs with $400M haul
summary: Including the heart drug developer’s offering, four biotechs in 2026 have raised at least $400 million in IPO proceeds — the most in a single year since 2021, according to BioPharma Dive data.

title: New antibiotic targets novel ribosomal site
Can multispecific biologics break the efficacy ceiling?
Multispecific T-cell engagers attract cancer drug developers

summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved the first generic of Xofluza (baloxavir marboxil) tablets, the first single-dose treatment for acute uncomplicated influenza and prophylaxis in…

title: UniQure to file gene therapy for approval, reflecting major shifts at FDA
summary: The decision marks another regulatory U-turn following the exits of Marty Makary and Vinay Prasad, suggesting to some analysts that current FDA leadership may be more flexible in certain cases.

title: Jazz zeroes in on next-gen T cell engagers with AbCellera deal
summary: The potentially $4 billion collaboration is the latest example of drugmakers working to broaden the use of T cell engagers, which, so far, have had limited success outside of certain blood cancers.

title: FDA Broadens Access to Over-the-Counter Naloxone Nasal Spray for Opioid Overdose
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) today approved another over-the-counter (OTC) intranasal naloxone product, Rextovy, a 4 milligram (mg) naloxone hydrochloride nasal spray for the emergency…

title: FDA Approves New Indication for Tzield (teplizumab) for Certain Pediatric Patients with Recently Diagnosed Stage 3 Type 1 Diabetes
summary: On June 12, 2026, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted accelerated approval to Tzield (teplizumab) for a new indication, to delay the decline of insulin production in pediatric patients ages…
